Home.statement: Здравствуйте! Я программист с {numYears}-летним опытом разработки приложений для Android и React. Мне нравится процесс итеративной доработки проектов и оттачивания мельчайших деталей до идеального результата. В свободное время я люблю играть в видеоигры, а также играть в футбол и смотреть футбольные матчи. Спасибо, что посетили мой сайт. Если у вас есть вопросы, не стесняйтесь обращаться ко мне!

Resume.android_about: Приложение позволяет пользователям отправлять формы, получать локальные и Acoustic push-уведомления о своих заявках, и использовать камеру устройства для сканирования VIN-кодов и фотографирование автомобилей.
Resume.android_analytics: Разработал набор отчетов Adobe Analytics и отправлял ежемесячные аналитические отчеты руководству
Resume.android_google_play: Обновил библиотеки Gradle и функциональность приложения в соответствии с требованиями Google Play
Resume.android_incidents: Разрешал инциденты в производственной среде ServiceNow, открытые конечными пользователями
Resume.android_msauth: Разработал аутентификацию Microsoft для соответствия новым требованиям безопасности
Resume.android_role: Руководитель разработки приложения на Jetpack Compose, Kotlin и Java с 17 000 пользователей в месяц
Resume.android_work_architecture: Реализованы плагины с использованием архитектуры MVI и инъекции зависимостей Hilt
Resume.android_work_build: Исправил проблемы с конвейерами CI/CD, запускаемыми через GitHub Actions
Resume.android_work_collab: Сотрудничал с командой разработчиков, тестировщиков QA и UAT, владельцев продуктов и специалистов по UI/UX для проектирования, определения объема и разработки новых функций
Resume.android_work_deployments: Выставлял тест версий приложение на Firebase и настоящие версий на Google Play
Resume.android_work_responsive: Разработал адаптивные макеты следуя рекомендациям по дизайну для смартфонов и для планшетов
Resume.android_work_submit: Пользователи могут отправлять данные по электронной почте и через асинхронную очередь, реализованную с помощью kotlinx. В среднем ежемесячно обрабатывается 15 000–20 000 заявок.
Resume.android_work_unitTests: Разработал набор модульных тестов с использованием Kotlinx Coroutines, JUnit и MockK
Resume.education_certificates: Сертификаты по новым медиа и науке о данных
Resume.personal_project_about: Next.js личный веб-сайт размещен на Vercel
Resume.personal_project_resume: Резюме для скачивания, реализованное с помощью @react/pdf-renderer
Resume.react_about: Приложение позволяет внутренним пользователям создавать и изменять проекты по возврату деталей, а также просматривать сводные таблицы существующих проектов
Resume.react_role: Typescript разработчик для перезаписи устаревшего приложения используя Nextjs микро_фронтенд,
Resume.react_work_api: Разработал API Redux RTK Query для интеграций с сервером
Resume.react_work_forms: Создал масштабируемые и надежные формы с помощью Formik, проверенные Yup
Resume.react_work_screens: Создал повторно используемые компоненты React и экраны на основе макетов Figma
Resume.react_work_unit_tests: Разработал тесты с использованием Jest и React Testing Library

Disclosures.attribution_next: <nextjs>Next.js</nextjs> для серверной прорисовки и организации структуры проекта.
Disclosures.attribution_react: <react>React</react> для рендеринга пользовательского интерфейса и построения архитектуры компонентов.
Disclosures.attribution_mui: <mui>MUI</mui> для компонентов дизайна, тематики и верстки.
Disclosures.attribution_next_intl: <nextIntl>next-intl</nextIntl> для локализации контента и переключения языков.
Disclosures.attribution_react_pdf: <pdfRenderer>@react/pdf-renderer</pdfRenderer> для генерации резюме в формате PDF, доступных для скачивания.
Disclosures.privacy_vercel_analytics: <vercelAnalytics>Vercel Analytics</vercelAnalytics> для сбора анонимных данных об использовании. Личные данные не собираются.
Disclosures.privacy_vercel_speed: <vercelSpeedInsights>Vercel Speed Insights</vercelSpeedInsights> и SpeedInsights для сбора анонимных данных об производительности. Личные данные не собираются.
Disclosures.privacy_cookies: Функциональные файлы cookie для сохранения ваших настроек темы и языка.
Disclosures.privacy_third_party: Сторонние библиотеки, у которых свои собственные лицензии и политики конфиденциальности.

Architecture.arch_struct_explanation: My codebase needs to be intuitive to navigate so I can easily make updates and modifications. It also needs to be scalable, readable, and follow best practices to demonstrate my competency as a developer.
Architecture.arch_default_description: Click a folder to see its children and a short description. Not every directory or file is represented - that felt excessive!
Architecture.arch_public_description: The Next.js convention is that anything in the public folder can be referenced anywhere in the codebase starting from the base url /. This is a good place to keep static files.
Architecture.arch_fonts_description: Here I store all the font files I use in the application as .ttf files. Because they're in 'public', I can access them anywhere in the app.
Architecture.arch_icons_description: Here I store a sprite file containing SVG icons used by my downloadable resume.
Architecture.arch_images_description: This folder contains all the images used in my app. This makes it easy for Next.js's Image components to access them - and for me to find them if I need to make an update.
Architecture.arch_strings_description: This directory stores localization files for each language: en.json, ru.json, etc. If I want to support a new language, I simply add a new json file here.
Architecture.arch_src_description: Next.js supports the common pattern of placing application code in a 'src' directory to separate it from configuration files (generally in the root directory).
Architecture.arch_app_description: This is the folder used by the Next.js app router. Every route in the site has to be declared in this directory.
Architecture.arch_app_architecture_description: This is the folder for the /architecture route - the screen that you're on now! It has its own layout.tsx and page.tsx.
Architecture.arch_app_disclosures_description: This is the folder for the /disclosures route - which you can view by opening the settings in the top right and clicking on the 'Disclosures' link. It also has its own layout.tsx and page.tsx.
Architecture.arch_favicon_description: The icon you see on the browser tab. Next.js convention dictates this icon is placed in the app/ directory.
Architecture.arch_layout_description: Every Next.js route needs a layout.tsx and a page.tsx. Since this is the root layout, it wraps all the other layouts, so in this file I wrap the app content in my theme, localization, and other providers.
Architecture.arch_page_description: This renders the actual content for the route: in this case, the home route at /. To keep the router lightweight, all I do here is call a react component home.tsx, which is stored in the 'components/home/' directory below.
Architecture.arch_notfound_description: This is a simple 404 page for any invalid route. Next.js handles this functionality by default, but I created this page so I could have a screen that was consistent with the look and feel of my app.
Architecture.arch_components_description: This folder contains the React components used to render the various screens of my application. The directory is split into sub-directories by screen and function.
Architecture.arch_components_architecture_description: For example, this folder contains all the React components specifically for the architecture screen - the screen you're on now.
Architecture.arch_projectstructure_description: And this file is the specific React Component for the project structure you're viewing now. There are different files for each of the different sections on this screen - given relevant names so it's easy to find and modify them when needed.
Architecture.arch_constants_description: This folder contains constant variables and lists used throughout the project, split into separate files by functionality. For example, my resume uses resumeConstants.tsx.
Architecture.arch_constants_arch_description: This file has all the constants used on the screen you're on now. For example, this project structure, an array of MockFile (see the 'types' directory), is defined here. If I wanted to display another folder, I'd add it to the array.
Architecture.arch_i18n_description: This folder contains a configuration file (request.ts) used to configure my localization library. Both the directory and the file have to have that exact name.
Architecture.arch_style_description: This folder contains the theming logic for my application. If I need to globally change the look and feel of the site, I will go here.
Architecture.arch_globals_description: This is a stlying file that needs to be imported in the root directory. Since I'm using MUI and Tailwind for my theming, all I'm doing in this file is importing the tailwind library.
Architecture.arch_theme_description: In this file, I configure my MUI theme (mainly the light and dark palettes) and I return a ThemeProvider that is wrapped around my application in the root layout.tsx file.
Architecture.arch_package_description: Various configuration files are placed into the root directory. For example, this file has all the third party npm dependencies that get installed when you run 'npm install'.
Architecture.arch_types_description: Here I have a types.tsx file where I export all the custom types I use in this project. For example, this simulated project directory is an array of MockFile. A MockFile consists of:
Architecture.arch_types_desc_title: title: string (The name of the directory or folder. In this case, 'types')
Architecture.arch_types_desc_ind: indentationLevel: number (Starts at 0, simulates project structure indentation. In this case, 1)
Architecture.arch_types_desc_desc: description: string (Either the string key directory's description, which fetches the value from the appropriate 'strings' file, or a constant to signify special formatting. In this case, the constant TYPES_DESCRIPTION)
Architecture.arch_types_desc_isFile: isFile: boolean? (Optional, to show the file icon. Not set for this directory.)
Architecture.arch_types_desc_path: path: string? (Optional, used to simulate the file explorer behavior by expanding and collapsing appropriate paths)
Architecture.arch_types_desc_final: Keeping all the types in one directory makes it easy to modify and maintain them. If the project grew large enough, I would split the types file by functionality, similar to how I manage the 'constants' and 'components' directories.
Architecture.arch_util_description: In this directory, I store utility functions that are used in multiple places in the app. For example, getCookie() and setCookie(), which are used to set the functional MUI and localization cookies.
Architecture.arch_perf_intro:
I wanted to write this website using React and various third party libraries to showcase my proficiency with them.

The downside of this approach, as opposed to a simple HTML page styled with CSS, is that React is pure Javascript. By default, Javascript renders on the user's browser, which means the page takes longer to load, especially the first time a user visits. This is a poor experience for any site, but especially for a personal site which should be fast and lightweight.

Therefore, I used Next.js for server-side rendering. I also did the following optimizations:
Architecture.arch_perf_builtin_desc: Next.js provides several components with various optimizations built in.
Architecture.arch_perf_font_desc: Bundled with static assets, improving privacy and performance
Architecture.arch_perf_font_desc1: Preloaded fonts prevent flashes of unstyled text
Architecture.arch_perf_font_desc2: No layout shift - fallback font has same font size
Architecture.arch_perf_dynamic_desc1: Load javascript modules and React components only when they're needed
Architecture.arch_perf_image_desc1: Only loads images when they enter the viewport - leads to faster page loading
Architecture.arch_perf_image_desc2: Prevents layout shift while images are loading
Architecture.arch_perf_testing_desc: To measure my performance in a more objective way than manual testing, I used the following tools:
Architecture.arch_perf_testing_lighthouse_desc1: Google Chrome has a built-in performance test.
Architecture.arch_perf_testing_lighthouse_desc2: Tested on both desktop and mobile devices.
Architecture.arch_perf_testing_lighthouse_desc3: Mobile tests will throttle your network to simulate a real user on their phone.
Architecture.arch_perf_testing_vercel_desc1: Provided by Vercel - the creators of Next.js and the hosting provider I used to deploy my website
Architecture.arch_perf_testing_vercel_desc2: Injected in the root layout.tsx file, tracks anonymized speed statistics of real users
Architecture.arch_perf_testing_vercel_desc3: Good to see how the website is actually performing in production
